title: OpenClaw cron-triggered late listener output should not crash the gateway

source.license_note: Localized ACBench reproduction derived from openclaw/openclaw issue #62876.

task.summary: Repair the listener guard so `cron run` residual output does not crash the gateway after the run ends.
task.instructions: Investigate why cron-driven late updates reach the listener after the active run is gone, repair the guard so those events are dropped safely, and preserve healthy in-run event handling.
task.acceptance_notes: Alternative fixes are allowed as long as late cron-triggered listener events stop crashing the runtime.

visible_context.issue_text: Running `openclaw cron run <job-id>` can hang and then crash the gateway with `Agent listener invoked outside active run` when late updates arrive.
error_logs: openclaw cron run triggers Unhandled promise rejection
late stderr/update reached Agent.processEvents after the run ended
reproduction_steps: Run the unit tests from the repository root.
visible_context.notes: This fixture reuses the localized late-listener guard bug with cron-style incident framing.

code_fault.description: Late cron-triggered listener updates are raised instead of skipped after the active run ends.

constraints.notes: This GitHub-derived reproduction is expected to be repaired in code, not by changing tests.

notes: Code-only localized reproduction of openclaw/openclaw issue #62876.
